what_it_does: Records two parked stranger-test copy refinements for a fork-owned merge-sa authorization decision card, showing current wording, refined wording, rationale, application plan, and closure when folded into a voice-elevation cutover.
observations: Two copy notes: sign-in-to-what clarity and explicit decide-later safety.
The file is closed as of 2026-06-19 and the refined strings were handed to the fork owner via channel t-1781832035.
It deliberately avoids speculative changes to the author's five cards until a stranger test raises the same issue.
It references a DNA renderArchetype cutover re-curate and an RC#3 elegance worklist as the fold trigger.
standouts: The 'its own key to the shared library, not yours' rewording of the sign-in implication.
The explicit 'No rush — not choosing changes nothing' decide-later language moved to the card's why field.
Closure happened during a voice-elevation pass rather than the originally expected wire cutover.
conventions: Decision-card note structure with CURRENT, REFINED, APPLY, and CLOSED sections.
References fork ownership, channel IDs, and cutover triggers.
Uses neutral, no-deferral language ('captured here NOW', 'no-deferral').
concerns: Some terms (DNA, RC#3, channel t-1781832035, 'my 5 cards') are not defined within the file.
Closure asserts 'fork applies + verifies' but the file itself does not contain verification evidence.
The 'Tim-altitude wording' reference is unexplained for readers without external context.
notes: A closed copy-refinement ticket for the merge-sa authorization decision card, preserving before/after wording and the handoff to the fork owner.
questions: What is the 'DNA renderArchetype CUTOVER re-curate'?
Which five cards are referred to as 'my 5 cards'?
What is RC#3 and the 'elegance worklist'?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to capture two parked stranger notes and fold them at the natural re-curate moment; the file does exactly that and records closure once the fold occurred.
apparent_themes: Explicit safe non-choice in decision copy
No speculative churn without a finding
intention_signals: It tries to prevent small but important UI copy findings from being dropped by parking them in a file-local ledger and supplying paste-ready strings to the owning fork at the right re-curate moment.
novelty: It treats a UI copy tweak as a formal 'parked stranger note' artifact with a lifecycle, a channel-based handoff, and a closure tied to a cutover re-curate.
contribution: It preserves the exact refined copy strings, the rationale for each wording change, the decision not to apply the same lens speculatively, and the closure/handoff trace, so a fused system can retain the copy decisions and their context.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a closed decision-card copy-refinement record for the merge-sa authorization option, containing two before/after wording changes and their handoff to the fork owner during a voice-elevation cutover.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to ensure two stranger-test copy findings about sign-in clarity and explicit decide-later language were captured immediately and folded into the fork-owned merge-sa card at the natural re-curate moment.
